title: 1.8 vs 1.9+ PvP
description: This data pack adds back 1.8 pvp while still keeping the option to stay in 1.9+ pvp. This lets you block with swords and changes saturation for each respective version. Players that have just joined are given an option to select their pvp version
body:
## Description
This data pack adds back 1.8 pvp while still keeping the option to stay in 1.9+ pvp. This lets you block with swords (1.8 only) and changes saturation for each respective version. Players that have just joined are given an option to select their pvp version. 1.9+ pvp stays around the same. 1.8 pvp players get sword blocking, no attack cooldowns, and weaker axes, but can't use their offhand.
## 1.8 pvp mechanics
  - Sword blocking
    - Roughly 50% damage reduction
    - Reduced knockback while airborne
  - No attack cooldowns
  - Old axe damage
  - No saturation healing
  - No offhand
  - Old Enchanted Golden Apples
## Config:
  - Offhand for 1.8
  - Shields for 1.8
  - Netherite for 1.8
  - Enchanted Golden Apple crafting for 1.8
  - 1.8 knockback
  - Natural Regeneration (for UHC)
  - Different ways to set pvp style
    - Player's choice (default)
    - All 1.8
    - All 1.9+ (at this point why did you download the pack?)
    - No automatic assigning
      - For use on servers that want to select for the players
  - Enchanted Golden Apple versions (1.8, 1.9+, bedrock)
### Note: 
Everything in this data pack functions as close to acutal Minecraft. Obtained through testing and information on the Minecraft wiki.

## For Multiplayer
For use on servers, set rate-limit=0 and network-compression-threshold=-1 in the server.properties file to advoid attack voiding. 1.8 PvP works best on low ping.

###  To-do:
  - Testing with a pro 1.8 player to find small quirks
  - Vertical Knockback while target is in the air (only while 1.8 players hit things)
  - No sweep hits for 1.8 players
